headline: Production evaluation for agentic systems breaks out, with human-in-the-loop and online evaluation rising alongside it.
body: The window centers on how agentic systems are evaluated and persisted in production, with human-in-the-loop, online evaluation, and structured workflows all entering or breaking out, anchored by Meta talks on continuous eval pipelines and drift detection and OpenGov's account of scaling an agent serving thousands of governments. Persistence is the recurring theme: separate talks frame durable session logs and persistent memory and context management as the differentiator as base models commoditize. On the model side, DeepReinforce's Ornith family brings open agentic coding models from 9B to 397B with self-learned scaffolding for planning, retries, and testing. Reasoning and test-time-compute also surface through recursive coding agent work.

cited_items.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
